post.description: Creates a new [Access Grant](/use-cases/granting-access/access-grants). Access Grants are the default and recommended way to grant a user access to any physical space, irrespective of the locking hardware. They work with both standalone smart locks (using `device_ids`) and access control systems (using `acs_entrance_ids` or `space_ids`), and can issue PIN codes, key cards, and mobile keys through a single request.

user_identity_id.description: ID of user identity for whom access is being granted.

user_identity.description: When used, creates a new user identity with the given details, and grants them access.

email_address.description: Unique email address for the user identity.

phone_number.description: Unique phone number for the user identity in [E.164 format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en) (for example, +15555550100).

user_identity_key.description: Unique key for the user identity.

access_grant_key.description: Unique key for the access grant within the workspace.

acs_entrance_ids.description: Set of IDs of the [entrances](/api/acs/systems/list) to which access is being granted.

customization_profile_id.description: ID of the customization profile to apply to the Access Grant and its access methods.

device_ids.description: Set of IDs of the [devices](/api/devices/list) to which access is being granted.

ends_at.description: Date and time at which the validity of the new grant ends, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format. Must be a time in the future and after `starts_at`.

location.description: When used, creates a new location with the given entrances and devices, and gives the user access to this location.

name.description: Name of the location.
Name for the access grant.

code.description: Specific PIN code to use for this access method. Only applicable when mode is 'code'.

instant_key_max_use_count.description: Maximum number of times the instant key can be used. Only applicable when mode is 'mobile_key'. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

reservation_key.description: Reservation key for the access grant.

space_ids.description: Set of IDs of existing spaces to which access is being granted.

space_keys.description: Set of keys of existing spaces to which access is being granted.

starts_at.description: Date and time at which the validity of the new grant starts, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

post.description: Adds additional requested access methods to an existing Access Grant.

access_grant_id.description: ID of the Access Grant to add access methods to.

requested_access_methods.description: Array of requested access methods to add to the access grant.

code.description: Specific PIN code to use for this access method. Only applicable when mode is 'code'.

instant_key_max_use_count.description: Maximum number of times the instant key can be used. Only applicable when mode is 'mobile_key'. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

post.summary: Add Requested Access Methods to Access Grant
post.x-title: Add Requested Access Methods to Access Grant

get.description: Get an unmanaged Access Grant (where is_managed = false).

schema.description: ID of unmanaged Access Grant to get.

access_grant.description: Represents an unmanaged Access Grant. Unmanaged Access Grants do not have client sessions, instant keys, customization profiles, or keys.

access_grant_id.description: ID of the Access Grant.

access_method_ids.description: IDs of the access methods created for the Access Grant.

created_at.description: Date and time at which the Access Grant was created.

display_name.description: Display name of the Access Grant.

ends_at.description: Date and time at which the Access Grant ends.

created_at.description: Date and time at which Seam created the error.

error_code.description: Unique identifier of the type of error. Enables quick recognition and categorization of the issue.

message.description: Detailed description of the error. Provides insights into the issue and potentially how to rectify it.

missing_device_ids.description: IDs of the devices that did not receive an access code at grant creation. Use these to identify which specific devices failed when the message reports a partial failure.

name.description: Name of the Access Grant. If not provided, the display name will be computed.

pending_mutations.description: List of pending mutations for the access grant. This shows updates that are in progress.

oneOf.description: Seam is in the process of updating the devices/spaces associated with this access grant.

created_at.description: Date and time at which the mutation was created.

device_ids.description: Previous device IDs where access codes existed.

message.description: Detailed description of the mutation.

mutation_code.description: Mutation code to indicate that Seam is in the process of updating the spaces (devices) associated with this access grant.

common_code_key.description: Common code key to ensure PIN code reuse across devices.

device_ids.description: New device IDs where access codes should be created.

oneOf.description: Seam is in the process of updating the access times for this access grant.

access_method_ids.description: IDs of the access methods being updated.

created_at.description: Date and time at which the mutation was created.

ends_at.description: Previous end time for access.

starts_at.description: Previous start time for access.

message.description: Detailed description of the mutation.

mutation_code.description: Mutation code to indicate that Seam is in the process of updating the access times for this access grant.

ends_at.description: New end time for access.

starts_at.description: New start time for access.

requested_access_methods.description: Access methods that the user requested for the Access Grant.

code.description: Specific PIN code to use for this access method. Only applicable when mode is 'code'.

created_access_method_ids.description: IDs of the access methods created for the requested access method.

created_at.description: Date and time at which the requested access method was added to the Access Grant.

display_name.description: Display name of the access method.

instant_key_max_use_count.description: Maximum number of times the instant key can be used. Only applicable when mode is 'mobile_key'. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

reservation_key.description: Reservation key for the access grant.

space_ids.description: IDs of the spaces to which the Access Grant gives access.

starts_at.description: Date and time at which the Access Grant starts.

user_identity_id.description: ID of user identity to which the Access Grant gives access.

items.description: Warning associated with the [access grant](/use-cases/granting-access).

oneOf.description: Indicates that the [access grant](/use-cases/granting-access) is being deleted.

message.description: Detailed description of the warning. Provides insights into the issue and potentially how to rectify it.

warning_code.description: Unique identifier of the type of warning. Enables quick recognition and categorization of the issue.

oneOf.description: Indicates that the access grant should have access to more locations than it currently does. Access methods are being created for the missing locations.

message.description: Detailed description of the warning. Provides insights into the issue and potentially how to rectify it.

patch.description:
Updates an unmanaged Access Grant to make it managed.

This endpoint can only be used to convert unmanaged access grants to managed ones by setting `is_managed` to `true`. It cannot be used to convert managed access grants back to unmanaged.

When converting an unmanaged access grant to managed, all associated access methods will also be converted to managed.

access_grant_id.description: ID of the unmanaged Access Grant to update.

access_grant_key.description: Unique key for the access grant. If not provided, the existing key will be preserved.

is_managed.description: Must be set to true to convert the unmanaged access grant to managed.

post.description:
Updates an unmanaged Access Grant to make it managed.

This endpoint can only be used to convert unmanaged access grants to managed ones by setting `is_managed` to `true`. It cannot be used to convert managed access grants back to unmanaged.

When converting an unmanaged access grant to managed, all associated access methods will also be converted to managed.

access_grant_id.description: ID of the unmanaged Access Grant to update.

access_grant_key.description: Unique key for the access grant. If not provided, the existing key will be preserved.

is_managed.description: Must be set to true to convert the unmanaged access grant to managed.

patch.description: Updates an existing Access Grant's time window.

access_grant_id.description: ID of the Access Grant to update.

ends_at.description: Date and time at which the validity of the grant ends, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format. Must be a time in the future and after `starts_at`.

name.description: Display name for the access grant.

starts_at.description: Date and time at which the validity of the grant starts, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

post.description: Updates an existing Access Grant's time window.

access_grant_id.description: ID of the Access Grant to update.

ends_at.description: Date and time at which the validity of the grant ends, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format. Must be a time in the future and after `starts_at`.

name.description: Display name for the access grant.

starts_at.description: Date and time at which the validity of the grant starts, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

post.description: Remotely unlocks a specified [entrance](/low-level-apis/access-systems/retrieving-entrance-details) using the cloud key credential associated with an access method. Returns an action attempt that tracks the progress of the unlock operation.

access_method_id.description: ID of the cloud_key `access_method` to use for the unlock operation.

acs_entrance_id.description: ID of the entrance to unlock.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

post.summary: Unlock a Door with an Access Method
post.x-title: Unlock a Door with an Access Method

get.description: Gets an unmanaged access method (where is_managed = false).

schema.description: ID of unmanaged access method to get.

access_method.description: Represents an unmanaged access method. Unmanaged access methods do not have client sessions, instant keys, customization profiles, or keys.

access_method_id.description: ID of the access method.

code.description: The actual PIN code for code access methods.

created_at.description: Date and time at which the access method was created.

display_name.description: Display name of the access method.

is_assignment_required.description: Indicates whether an existing card credential must be assigned to this access method before it can be issued. Only applies to card-mode access methods on systems that support credential assignment.

is_encoding_required.description: Indicates whether encoding with an card encoder is required to issue or reissue the plastic card associated with the access method.

is_issued.description: Indicates whether the access method has been issued.

is_ready_for_assignment.description: Indicates whether the access method is ready for card assignment. This is true when the access method is in card mode, has not yet been issued, and the system supports credential assignment.

is_ready_for_encoding.description: Indicates whether the access method is ready to be encoded. This is true when the credential has been created and the card has not yet been issued.

issued_at.description: Date and time at which the access method was issued.

pending_mutations.description: Pending mutations for the [access method](/use-cases/granting-access/delivering-access-methods). Indicates operations that are in progress.

oneOf.description: Seam is in the process of provisioning access for this access method on new devices.

created_at.description: Date and time at which the mutation was created.

device_ids.description: Previous device IDs where access was provisioned.

message.description: Detailed description of the mutation.

mutation_code.description: Mutation code to indicate that Seam is in the process of provisioning access for this access method on new devices.

device_ids.description: New device IDs where access is being provisioned.

oneOf.description: Seam is in the process of revoking access for this access method from devices.

created_at.description: Date and time at which the mutation was created.

device_ids.description: Previous device IDs where access existed.

message.description: Detailed description of the mutation.

mutation_code.description: Mutation code to indicate that Seam is in the process of revoking access for this access method from devices.

device_ids.description: New device IDs where access should remain.

oneOf.description: Seam is in the process of updating the access times for this access method.

created_at.description: Date and time at which the mutation was created.

ends_at.description: Previous end time for access.

starts_at.description: Previous start time for access.

message.description: Detailed description of the mutation.

mutation_code.description: Mutation code to indicate that Seam is in the process of updating the access times for this access method.

ends_at.description: New end time for access.

starts_at.description: New start time for access.

items.description: Warning associated with the [access method](/use-cases/granting-access/delivering-access-methods).

oneOf.description: Indicates that the [access method](/use-cases/granting-access/delivering-access-methods) is being deleted.

created_at.description: Date and time at which Seam created the warning.

message.description: Detailed description of the warning. Provides insights into the issue and potentially how to rectify it.

warning_code.description: Unique identifier of the type of warning. Enables quick recognition and categorization of the issue.

oneOf.description: Indicates that the access times for this [access method](/use-cases/granting-access/delivering-access-methods) are being updated.

created_at.description: Date and time at which Seam created the warning.

message.description: Detailed description of the warning. Provides insights into the issue and potentially how to rectify it.

warning_code.description: Unique identifier of the type of warning. Enables quick recognition and categorization of the issue.

oneOf.description: Indicates that all attempts to create an access code on this device before the start time failed and a backup access code was used to ensure access was provided in time.

created_at.description: Date and time at which Seam created the warning.

message.description: Detailed description of the warning. Provides insights into the issue and potentially how to rectify it.

original_access_method_id.description: ID of the original access method from which this backup access method was split, if applicable.

workspace_id.description: ID of the Seam workspace associated with the access method.

post.description: Adds a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) to a specified [access group](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management/assigning-users-to-access-groups).

acs_access_group_id.description: ID of the access group to which you want to add an access system user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to add to an access group. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the desired user identity that you want to add to an access group. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id. If the ACS system contains an ACS user with the same `email_address` or `phone_number` as the user identity that you specify, they are linked, and the access group membership belongs to the ACS user. If the ACS system does not have a corresponding ACS user, one is created.

post.summary: Add an ACS User to an Access Group
post.x-title: Add an ACS User to an Access Group

If the ACS system contains an ACS user with the same `email_address` or `phone_number` as the user identity that you specify, they are linked, and the access group membership belongs to the ACS user. x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --include --request POST "https://connect.getseam.com/acs/access_groups/get" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $SEAM_API_KEY" \
  --json @- <<EOF
{
  "acs_access_group_id": "09eb5265-6e3b-4e6d-bf96-736171c547ae"
}
EOF

get.description: Returns a list of all [access groups](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management/assigning-users-to-access-groups).

schema.description: ID of the access system for which you want to retrieve all access groups.
ID of the access system user for which you want to retrieve all access groups.
ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access groups.
String for which to search. Filters returned access groups to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `name` or `acs_access_group_id`.

post.description: Returns a list of all [access groups](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management/assigning-users-to-access-groups).

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system for which you want to retrieve all access groups.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user for which you want to retrieve all access groups.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned access groups to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `name` or `acs_access_group_id`.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access groups.

delete.description: Removes a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) from a specified [access group](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management/assigning-users-to-access-groups).

schema.description: ID of the access group from which you want to remove an access system user.
ID of the access system user that you want to remove from an access group.
ID of the user identity associated with the user that you want to remove from an access group.

delete.x-title: Remove an ACS User from an Access Group

post.description: Removes a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) from a specified [access group](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management/assigning-users-to-access-groups).

acs_access_group_id.description: ID of the access group from which you want to remove an access system user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to remove from an access group.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity associated with the user that you want to remove from an access group.

post.summary: Remove an ACS User from an Access Group
post.x-title: Remove an ACS User from an Access Group

patch.description: Assigns a specified [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) to a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the credential that you want to assign to an access system user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user to whom you want to assign a credential. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity to whom you want to assign a credential. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id. If the ACS system contains an ACS user with the same `email_address` or `phone_number` as the user identity that you specify, they are linked, and the credential belongs to the ACS user. If the ACS system does not have a corresponding ACS user, one is created.

patch.x-title: Assign a Credential to an ACS User

post.description: Assigns a specified [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) to a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the credential that you want to assign to an access system user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user to whom you want to assign a credential. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity to whom you want to assign a credential. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id. If the ACS system contains an ACS user with the same `email_address` or `phone_number` as the user identity that you specify, they are linked, and the credential belongs to the ACS user. If the ACS system does not have a corresponding ACS user, one is created.

post.summary: Assign a Credential to an ACS User
post.x-title: Assign a Credential to an ACS User

post.description: Creates a new [credential](/low-level-apis/managing-credentials) for a specified [ACS user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management). For granting access, we recommend [Access Grants](/use-cases/granting-access) instead: they create and manage the underlying credentials for you, across access systems and standalone smart locks alike. Use this low-level endpoint only when you need direct control over an individual ACS credential.

access_method.description: Access method for the new credential. Supported values: `code`, `card`, `mobile_key`, `cloud_key`.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system to which the new credential belongs. You must provide either `acs_user_id` or the combination of `user_identity_id` and `acs_system_id`.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user to whom the new credential belongs. You must provide either `acs_user_id` or the combination of `user_identity_id` and `acs_system_id`.

allowed_acs_entrance_ids.description: Set of IDs of the [entrances](/low-level-apis/access-systems/retrieving-entrance-details) for which the new credential grants access.

assa_abloy_vostio_metadata.description: Vostio-specific metadata for the new credential.

code.description: Access (PIN) code for the new credential. There may be manufacturer-specific code restrictions. For details, see the applicable [device or system integration guide](/device-and-system-integration-guides).

credential_manager_acs_system_id.description: ACS system ID of the credential manager for the new credential.

ends_at.description: Date and time at which the validity of the new credential ends, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format. Must be a time in the future and after `starts_at`.

is_multi_phone_sync_credential.description: Indicates whether the new credential is a [multi-phone sync credential](/capability-guides/mobile-access/issuing-mobile-credentials-from-an-access-control-system#what-are-multi-phone-sync-credentials).

salto_space_metadata.description: Salto Space-specific metadata for the new credential.

assign_new_key.description: Indicates whether to assign a first, new card to a user. See also [Programming Salto Space Card-based Credentials](/device-and-system-integration-guides/salto-proaccess-space-access-system/programming-salto-space-card-based-credentials).

update_current_key.description: Indicates whether to update the user's existing card. See also [Programming Salto Space Card-based Credentials](/device-and-system-integration-guides/salto-proaccess-space-access-system/programming-salto-space-card-based-credentials).

starts_at.description: Date and time at which the validity of the new credential starts, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity to whom the new credential belongs. You must provide either `acs_user_id` or the combination of `user_identity_id` and `acs_system_id`. If the access system contains a user with the same `email_address` or `phone_number` as the user identity that you specify, they are linked, and the credential belongs to the access system user. If the access system does not have a corresponding user, one is created.

visionline_metadata.description: Visionline-specific metadata for the new credential.

assa_abloy_credential_service_mobile_end.x-undocumented: Deprecated. Read-only endpoint references moved to `endpoint`.

get.description: Returns a list of all [credentials](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials).

post.description: Returns a list of all [credentials](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials).

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user for which you want to retrieve all credentials.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system for which you want to retrieve all credentials.
ID of the access system for which you want to retrieve all credentials.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user for which you want to retrieve all credentials.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all credentials.

created_before.description: Date and time, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format, before which events to return were created.

is_multi_phone_sync_credential.description: Indicates whether you want to retrieve only multi-phone sync credentials or non-multi-phone sync credentials.

limit.description: Number of credentials to return.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned credentials to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `display_name`, `code`, `card_number`, `acs_user_id` or `acs_credential_id`.

patch.description: Unassigns a specified [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) from a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the credential that you want to unassign from an access system user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user from which you want to unassign a credential. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity from which you want to unassign a credential. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

patch.x-title: Unassign a Credential from an ACS User

post.description: Unassigns a specified [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) from a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the credential that you want to unassign from an access system user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user from which you want to unassign a credential. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity from which you want to unassign a credential. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

post.summary: Unassign a Credential from an ACS User
post.x-title: Unassign a Credential from an ACS User

patch.description: Updates the code and ends at date and time for a specified [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials).

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the credential that you want to update.

code.description: Replacement access (PIN) code for the credential that you want to update.

ends_at.description: Replacement date and time at which the validity of the credential ends, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format. Must be a time in the future and after the `starts_at` value that you set when creating the credential.

post.description: Updates the code and ends at date and time for a specified [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials).

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the credential that you want to update.

code.description: Replacement access (PIN) code for the credential that you want to update.

ends_at.description: Replacement date and time at which the validity of the credential ends, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format. Must be a time in the future and after the `starts_at` value that you set when creating the credential.

x-codeSamples.label: Seam SDK

post.description: Encodes an existing [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) onto a plastic card placed on the specified [encoder](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners). Either provide an `acs_credential_id` or an `access_method_id`

access_method_id.description: ID of the `access_method` to encode onto a card.

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the `acs_credential` to encode onto a card.

acs_encoder_id.description: ID of the `acs_encoder` to use to encode the `acs_credential`.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

get.description: Returns a specified [encoder](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners).

schema.description: ID of the encoder that you want to get.

post.description: Returns a specified [encoder](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners).

acs_encoder_id.description: ID of the encoder that you want to get.

get.description: Returns a list of all [encoders](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners).

post.description: Returns a list of all [encoders](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners).

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system for which you want to retrieve all encoders.

acs_system_ids.description: IDs of the access systems for which you want to retrieve all encoders.

acs_encoder_ids.description: IDs of the encoders that you want to retrieve.

limit.description: Number of encoders to return.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

post.description: Scans an encoded [acs_credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) from a plastic card placed on the specified [encoder](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners).

acs_encoder_id.description: ID of the encoder to use for the scan.

salto_ks_metadata.description: Salto KS-specific metadata for the scan action.

detect_new_tags.description: When true, activates tag registration mode on the encoder to detect new, unregistered tags. When false, only detects existing tags already registered in the system. Defaults to false.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

post.description: Scans a physical card placed on the specified [encoder](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners) and assigns the scanned credential to an ACS user. Provide either an `acs_user_id` or a `user_identity_id`.

acs_encoder_id.description: ID of the `acs_encoder` to use to scan the credential.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the `acs_user` to assign the scanned credential to.

salto_ks_metadata.description: Salto KS-specific metadata for the scan action.

detect_new_tags.description: When true, activates tag registration mode on the encoder to detect new, unregistered tags. When false, only detects existing tags already registered in the system. Defaults to false.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the `user_identity` to assign the scanned credential to. If the ACS system contains an ACS user linked to this user identity, it is used. Otherwise, one is created.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

post.description: Simulates that the next attempt to encode a [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) using the specified [encoder](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners) will fail. You can only perform this action within a [sandbox workspace](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces).

acs_encoder_id.description: ID of the `acs_encoder` that will be used in the next request to encode the `acs_credential`.

error_code.description: Code of the error to simulate.

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the `acs_credential` that will fail to be encoded onto a card in the next request.

acs_encoder_id.description: ID of the `acs_encoder` that will be used in the next request to encode the `acs_credential`.

error_code.description: Code of the error to simulate.

post.summary: Simulate that the Next Credential Encoding Will Fail
post.x-title: Simulate that the Next Credential Encoding Will Fail

post.description: Simulates that the next attempt to encode a [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) using the specified [encoder](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners) will succeed. You can only perform this action within a [sandbox workspace](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces).

acs_encoder_id.description: ID of the `acs_encoder` that will be used in the next request to encode the `acs_credential`.

post.summary: Simulate that the Next Credential Encoding Will Succeed
post.x-title: Simulate that the Next Credential Encoding Will Succeed

post.description: Simulates that the next attempt to scan a [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) using the specified [encoder](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners) will fail. You can only perform this action within a [sandbox workspace](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces).

acs_encoder_id.description: ID of the `acs_encoder` that will fail to scan the `acs_credential` in the next request.
ID of the `acs_encoder` that will fail to scan the `acs_credential` in the next request.

post.summary: Simulate that the Next Credential Scan Will Fail
post.x-title: Simulate that the Next Credential Scan Will Fail

post.description: Simulates that the next attempt to scan a [credential](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials) using the specified [encoder](/low-level-apis/access-systems/working-with-card-encoders-and-scanners) will succeed. You can only perform this action within a [sandbox workspace](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces).

acs_credential_id_on_seam.description: ID of the Seam `acs_credential` that matches the `acs_credential` on the encoder in this simulation.

acs_encoder_id.description: ID of the `acs_encoder` that will be used in the next request to scan the `acs_credential`.
ID of the `acs_encoder` that will be used in the next request to scan the `acs_credential`.

post.description: Grants a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) access to a specified [access system entrance](/low-level-apis/access-systems/retrieving-entrance-details).

acs_entrance_id.description: ID of the entrance to which you want to grant an access system user access.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user to whom you want to grant access to an entrance. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity to whom you want to grant access to an entrance. You can only provide one of acs_user_id or user_identity_id. If the ACS system contains an ACS user with the same `email_address` or `phone_number` as the user identity that you specify, they are linked, and the access group membership belongs to the ACS user. If the ACS system does not have a corresponding ACS user, one is created.

post.summary: Grant an ACS User Access to an Entrance
post.x-title: Grant an ACS User Access to an Entrance

get.description: Returns a list of all [access system entrances](/low-level-apis/access-systems/retrieving-entrance-details).

schema.description: Customer key for which you want to list entrances.
ID of the access system for which you want to retrieve all entrances.
ID of the credential for which you want to retrieve all entrances.
ID of the space for which you want to list entrances.
ID of the connected account for which you want to retrieve all entrances.
IDs of the entrances for which you want to retrieve all entrances.
String for which to search. Filters returned entrances to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `display_name`.
Maximum number of records to return per page.
Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

post.description: Returns a list of all [access system entrances](/low-level-apis/access-systems/retrieving-entrance-details).

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the credential for which you want to retrieve all entrances.

acs_entrance_ids.description: IDs of the entrances for which you want to retrieve all entrances.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system for which you want to retrieve all entrances.

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account for which you want to retrieve all entrances.

customer_key.description: Customer key for which you want to list entrances.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned entrances to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `display_name`.

space_id.description: ID of the space for which you want to list entrances.

post.description: Remotely unlocks a specified [entrance](/low-level-apis/access-systems/retrieving-entrance-details) using a cloud_key credential. Returns an action attempt that tracks the progress of the unlock operation.

acs_credential_id.description: ID of the cloud_key credential to use for the unlock operation.

acs_entrance_id.description: ID of the entrance to unlock.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

get.description: Returns a specified [access system](/low-level-apis/access-systems).

schema.description: ID of the access system that you want to get.

post.description: Returns a specified [access system](/low-level-apis/access-systems).

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system that you want to get.

get.description:
Returns a list of all [access systems](/low-level-apis/access-systems).

To filter the list of returned access systems by a specific connected account ID, include the `connected_account_id` in the request body. If you omit the `connected_account_id` parameter, the response includes all access systems connected to your workspace.

schema.description: Customer key for which you want to list access systems.
ID of the connected account by which you want to filter the list of access systems.
String for which to search. Filters returned access systems to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `name` or `acs_system_id`.

post.description:
Returns a list of all [access systems](/low-level-apis/access-systems).

To filter the list of returned access systems by a specific connected account ID, include the `connected_account_id` in the request body. If you omit the `connected_account_id` parameter, the response includes all access systems connected to your workspace.

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account by which you want to filter the list of access systems.

customer_key.description: Customer key for which you want to list access systems.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned access systems to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `name` or `acs_system_id`.

get.description:
Returns a list of all credential manager systems that are compatible with a specified [access system](/low-level-apis/access-systems).

Specify the access system for which you want to retrieve all compatible credential manager systems by including the corresponding `acs_system_id` in the request body.

schema.description: ID of the access system for which you want to retrieve all compatible credential manager systems.

get.x-title: List Compatible Credential Manager ACS Systems

post.description:
Returns a list of all credential manager systems that are compatible with a specified [access system](/low-level-apis/access-systems).

Specify the access system for which you want to retrieve all compatible credential manager systems by including the corresponding `acs_system_id` in the request body.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system for which you want to retrieve all compatible credential manager systems.

post.summary: List Compatible Credential Manager ACS Systems
post.x-title: List Compatible Credential Manager ACS Systems

post.description: Reports ACS system device status including encoders and entrances.

acs_encoders.description: Array of ACS encoders to report

hotek_metadata.description: Hotek-specific metadata associated with the entrance.

encoder_number.description: The encoder number determined by the USB port connection.

acs_entrances.description: Array of ACS entrances to report

hotek_metadata.description: Hotek-specific metadata associated with the entrance.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the ACS system to report resources for

post.description: Adds a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) to a specified [access group](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management/assigning-users-to-access-groups).

acs_access_group_id.description: ID of the access group to which you want to add an access system user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to add to an access group.

post.summary: Add an ACS User to an Access Group
post.x-title: Add an ACS User to an Access Group

put.description: Adds a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) to a specified [access group](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management/assigning-users-to-access-groups).

acs_access_group_id.description: ID of the access group to which you want to add an access system user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to add to an access group.

put.x-title: Add an ACS User to an Access Group

post.description: Creates a new [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

access_schedule.description: `starts_at` and `ends_at` timestamps for the new access system user's access. If you specify an `access_schedule`, you may include both `starts_at` and `ends_at`. If you omit `starts_at`, it defaults to the current time. `ends_at` is optional and must be a time in the future and after `starts_at`.

ends_at.description: Ending timestamp for the new access system user's access.

starts_at.description: Starting timestamp for the new access system user's access.

acs_access_group_ids.description: Array of access group IDs to indicate the access groups to which you want to add the new access system user.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system to which you want to add the new access system user.

email_address.description: Email address of the [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

full_name.description: Full name of the new access system user.

phone_number.description: Phone number of the [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) in E.164 format (for example, `+15555550100`).

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity with which you want to associate the new access system user.

delete.description: Deletes a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) and invalidates the access system user's [credentials](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials).

schema.description: ID of the access system user that you want to delete. You must provide either acs_user_id or user_identity_id
ID of the user identity that you want to delete. You must provide either acs_user_id or user_identity_id. If you provide user_identity_id, you must also provide acs_system_id.
ID of the access system that you want to delete. You must provide acs_system_id with user_identity_id.

post.description: Deletes a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) and invalidates the access system user's [credentials](/low-level-apis/access-systems/managing-credentials).

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system that you want to delete. You must provide acs_system_id with user_identity_id.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to delete. You must provide either acs_user_id or user_identity_id

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to delete. You must provide either acs_user_id or user_identity_id. If you provide user_identity_id, you must also provide acs_system_id.

get.description: Returns a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

schema.description: ID of the access system user that you want to get. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.
ID of the user identity that you want to get. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.
ID of the access system that you want to get. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to get. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to get. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

get.description: Returns a list of all [access system users](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

schema.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access system users.
Phone number of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access system users, in [E.164 format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en) (for example, `+15555550100`).
Email address of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access system users.
ID of the `acs_system` for which you want to retrieve all access system users.

String for which to search. Filters returned access system users to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `full_name`, `phone_number`, `email_address`, `acs_user_id`, `user_identity_id`, `user_identity_full_name` or `user_identity_phone_number`.
Maximum number of records to return per page.
Timestamp by which to limit returned access system users. Returns users created before this timestamp.
Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

post.description: Returns a list of all [access system users](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

acs_system_id.description: ID of the `acs_system` for which you want to retrieve all access system users.

created_before.description: Timestamp by which to limit returned access system users. Returns users created before this timestamp.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned access system users to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `full_name`, `phone_number`, `email_address`, `acs_user_id`, `user_identity_id`, `user_identity_full_name` or `user_identity_phone_number`.

user_identity_email_address.description: Email address of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access system users.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access system users.

user_identity_phone_number.description: Phone number of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access system users, in [E.164 format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en) (for example, `+15555550100`).

get.description: Lists the [entrances](/api/acs/entrances) to which a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) has access.

schema.description: ID of the access system user for whom you want to list accessible entrances. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.
ID of the user identity for whom you want to list accessible entrances. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.
ID of the access system for which you want to list accessible entrances. You can only provide acs_system_id with user_identity_id.

acs_access_group_id.description: ID of the access group from which you want to remove an access system user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to remove from an access group. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

post.x-title: Remove an ACS User from an Access Group

post.description: Revokes access to all [entrances](/api/acs/entrances) for a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system for which you want to revoke access. You can only provide acs_system_id with user_identity_id.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user for whom you want to revoke access. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity for whom you want to revoke access. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

post.x-title: Revoke ACS User Access to All Entrances

post.description: [Suspends](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management/suspending-and-unsuspending-users#suspend-an-acs-user) a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management). Suspending an access system user revokes their access temporarily. To restore an access system user's access, you can [unsuspend](/api/acs/users/unsuspend) them.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system that you want to suspend. You can only provide acs_user_id or the combination of acs_system_id and user_identity_id.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to suspend. You can only provide acs_user_id or the combination of acs_system_id and user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to suspend. You can only provide acs_user_id or the combination of acs_system_id and user_identity_id.

post.description: [Unsuspends](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management/suspending-and-unsuspending-users#unsuspend-an-acs-user) a specified suspended [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management). While [suspending an access system user](/api/acs/users/suspend) revokes their access temporarily, unsuspending the access system user restores their access.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system of the user that you want to unsuspend. You can only provide acs_system_id with user_identity_id.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to unsuspend. You can only provide acs_user_id or the combination of acs_system_id and user_identity_id.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to unsuspend. You can only provide acs_user_id or the combination of acs_system_id and user_identity_id.

patch.description: Updates the properties of a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

access_schedule.description: `starts_at` and `ends_at` timestamps for the access system user's access. If you specify an `access_schedule`, you may include both `starts_at` and `ends_at`. If you omit `starts_at`, it defaults to the current time. `ends_at` is optional and must be a time in the future and after `starts_at`.

ends_at.description: Ending timestamp for the access system user's access.

starts_at.description: Starting timestamp for the access system user's access.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system that you want to update. You can only provide acs_system_id with user_identity_id.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to update. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

email_address.description: Email address of the [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

phone_number.description: Phone number of the [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) in E.164 format (for example, `+15555550100`).

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to update. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id. If you provide user_identity_id, you must also provide acs_system_id.

post.description: Updates the properties of a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

If you omit `starts_at`, it defaults to the current time. `ends_at` is optional and must be a time in the future and after `starts_at`.

ends_at.description: Ending timestamp for the access system user's access.

starts_at.description: Starting timestamp for the access system user's access.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system that you want to update. You can only provide acs_system_id with user_identity_id.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to update. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id.

email_address.description: Email address of the [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management).

phone_number.description: Phone number of the [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) in E.164 format (for example, `+15555550100`).

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to update. You can only provide acs_user_id or user_identity_id. If you provide user_identity_id, you must also provide acs_system_id.

post.description: Creates a new [client session](/core-concepts/authentication/client-session-tokens).

connect_webview_ids.description: IDs of the [Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews) for which you want to create a client session.

connected_account_ids.description: IDs of the [connected accounts](/core-concepts/connected-accounts) for which you want to create a client session.

customer_id.description: Customer ID that you want to associate with the new client session.

customer_key.description: Customer key that you want to associate with the new client session.

expires_at.description: Date and time at which the client session should expire, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

user_identifier_key.description: Your user ID for the user for whom you want to create a client session.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) for which you want to create a client session.

user_identity_ids.description: IDs of the [user identities](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) that you want to associate with the client session.

client_session_id.description: ID of the client session to which you want to grant access to resources.

connected_account_ids.description: IDs of the [connected accounts](/core-concepts/connected-accounts) that you want to associate with the client session.

user_identifier_key.description: Your user ID for the user that you want to associate with the client session.

user_identity_ids.description: IDs of the [user identities](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) that you want to associate with the client session.

schema.description: ID of the client session that you want to retrieve.

Indicates whether to retrieve only client sessions without associated user identifier keys.
ID of the [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) for which you want to retrieve client sessions.

post.description: Returns a list of all [client sessions](/core-concepts/authentication/client-session-tokens).

client_session_id.description: ID of the client session that you want to retrieve.

connect_webview_id.description: ID of the [Connect Webview](/core-concepts/connect-webviews) for which you want to retrieve client sessions.

user_identifier_key.description: Your user ID for the user by which you want to filter client sessions.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) for which you want to retrieve client sessions.

without_user_identifier_key.description: Indicates whether to retrieve only client sessions without associated user identifier keys.

post.description:
Revokes a [client session](/core-concepts/authentication/client-session-tokens).

Note that [deleting a client session](/api/client_sessions/delete) is a separate action.

client_session_id.description: ID of the client session that you want to revoke.

post.description:
Creates a new [Connect Webview](/core-concepts/connect-webviews).

To enable a user to connect their devices or systems to Seam, they must sign in to their device or system account. To enable a user to sign in, you create a `connect_webview`. After creating the Connect Webview, you receive a URL that you can use to display the visual component of this Connect Webview for your user. You can open an iframe or new window to display the Connect Webview.

You should make a new `connect_webview` for each unique login request. Each `connect_webview` tracks the user that signed in with it. You receive an error if you reuse a Connect Webview for the same user twice or if you use the same Connect Webview for multiple users.

See also: [Connect Webview Process](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/connect-webview-process).

accepted_capabilities.description: List of accepted device capabilities that restrict the types of devices that can be connected through the Connect Webview. If not provided, defaults will be determined based on the accepted providers.

items.description:
High-level device capabilities that can be restricted in connect webviews.
  These represent the main device categories that customers can opt into.

accepted_providers.description: Accepted device provider keys as an alternative to `provider_category`. Use this parameter to specify accepted providers explicitly. See [Customize the Brands to Display in Your Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/customizing-connect-webviews#customize-the-brands-to-display-in-your-connect-webviews). To list all provider keys, use [`/devices/list_device_providers`](/api/devices/list_device_providers) with no filters.

automatically_manage_new_devices.description: Indicates whether newly-added devices should appear as [managed devices](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices). See also: [Customize the Behavior Settings of Your Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/customizing-connect-webviews#customize-the-behavior-settings-of-your-connect-webviews).

custom_metadata.description: Custom metadata that you want to associate with the Connect Webview. Supports up to 50 JSON key:value pairs. [Adding custom metadata to a Connect Webview](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/attaching-custom-data-to-the-connect-webview) enables you to store custom information, like customer details or internal IDs from your application. The custom metadata is then transferred to any [connected accounts](/core-concepts/connected-accounts) that were connected using the Connect Webview, making it easy to find and filter these resources in your [workspace](/core-concepts/workspaces). You can also [filter Connect Webviews by custom metadata](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/filtering-connect-webviews-by-custom-metadata).

custom_redirect_failure_url.description: Alternative URL that you want to redirect the user to on an error. If you do not set this parameter, the Connect Webview falls back to the `custom_redirect_url`.

custom_redirect_url.description: URL that you want to redirect the user to after the provider login is complete.

customer_key.description: Associate the Connect Webview, the connected account, and all resources under the connected account with a customer. If the connected account already exists, it will be associated with the customer. If the connected account already exists, but is already associated with a customer, the Connect Webview will show an error.

excluded_providers.description: List of provider keys to exclude from the Connect Webview. These providers will not be shown when the user tries to connect an account.

provider_category.description: Specifies the category of providers that you want to include. To list all providers within a category, use [`/devices/list_device_providers`](/api/devices/list_device_providers) with the desired `provider_category` filter.

wait_for_device_creation.description: Indicates whether Seam should finish syncing all devices in a newly-connected account before completing the associated Connect Webview. See also: [Customize the Behavior Settings of Your Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/customizing-connect-webviews#customize-the-behavior-settings-of-your-connect-webviews).

delete.description:
Deletes a [Connect Webview](/core-concepts/connect-webviews).

You do not need to delete a Connect Webview once a user completes it. Instead, you can simply ignore completed Connect Webviews.

connect_webview_id.description: ID of the Connect Webview that you want to delete.

post.description:
Deletes a [Connect Webview](/core-concepts/connect-webviews).

You do not need to delete a Connect Webview once a user completes it. Instead, you can simply ignore completed Connect Webviews.

connect_webview_id.description: ID of the Connect Webview that you want to delete.

get.description:
Returns a specified [Connect Webview](/core-concepts/connect-webviews).

Unless you're using a `custom_redirect_url`, you should poll a newly-created `connect_webview` to find out if the user has signed in or to get details about what devices they've connected.

schema.description: ID of the Connect Webview that you want to get.

post.description:
Returns a specified [Connect Webview](/core-concepts/connect-webviews).

Unless you're using a `custom_redirect_url`, you should poll a newly-created `connect_webview` to find out if the user has signed in or to get details about what devices they've connected.

connect_webview_id.description: ID of the Connect Webview that you want to get.

get.description: Returns a list of all [Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews).

schema.description: Customer key for which you want to list connect webviews.
Your user ID for the user by which you want to filter Connect Webviews.

Custom metadata pairs by which you want to [filter Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/filtering-connect-webviews-by-custom-metadata). Returns Connect Webviews with `custom_metadata` that contains all of the provided key:value pairs.

String for which to search. Filters returned Connect Webviews to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `connect_webview_id`, `accepted_providers`, `custom_metadata`, or `customer_key`.
Maximum number of records to return per page.
Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

post.description: Returns a list of all [Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews).

custom_metadata_has.description: Custom metadata pairs by which you want to [filter Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/filtering-connect-webviews-by-custom-metadata). Returns Connect Webviews with `custom_metadata` that contains all of the provided key:value pairs.

customer_key.description: Customer key for which you want to list connect webviews.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned Connect Webviews to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `connect_webview_id`, `accepted_providers`, `custom_metadata`, or `customer_key`.

user_identifier_key.description: Your user ID for the user by which you want to filter Connect Webviews.

delete.description:
Deletes a specified [connected account](/core-concepts/connected-accounts).

Deleting a connected account triggers a `connected_account.deleted` event and removes the connected account and all data associated with the connected account from Seam, including devices, events, access codes, and so on. For every deleted resource, Seam sends a corresponding deleted event, but the resource is not deleted from the provider.

For example, if you delete a connected account with a device that has an access code, Seam sends a `connected_account.deleted` event, a `device.deleted` event, and an `access_code.deleted` event, but Seam does not remove the access code from the device.

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account that you want to delete.

post.description:
Deletes a specified [connected account](/core-concepts/connected-accounts).

Deleting a connected account triggers a `connected_account.deleted` event and removes the connected account and all data associated with the connected account from Seam, including devices, events, access codes, and so on. For every deleted resource, Seam sends a corresponding deleted event, but the resource is not deleted from the provider.

For example, if you delete a connected account with a device that has an access code, Seam sends a `connected_account.deleted` event, a `device.deleted` event, and an `access_code.deleted` event, but Seam does not remove the access code from the device.

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account that you want to delete.

get.description: Returns a specified [connected account](/core-concepts/connected-accounts).

post.description: Returns a specified [connected account](/core-concepts/connected-accounts).

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account that you want to get.

email.description: Email address associated with the connected account that you want to get.

get.description: Returns a list of all [connected accounts](/core-concepts/connected-accounts).

schema.description: Your user ID for the user by which you want to filter connected accounts.
Custom metadata pairs by which you want to filter connected accounts. Returns connected accounts with `custom_metadata` that contains all of the provided key:value pairs.
Customer key by which you want to filter connected accounts.
ID of the space by which you want to filter connected accounts.

String for which to search. Filters returned connected accounts to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `connected_account_id`, `account_type`, `customer_key`, `custom_metadata`, `user_identifier.username`, `user_identifier.email` or `user_identifier.phone`.
Maximum number of records to return per page.
Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

post.description: Returns a list of all [connected accounts](/core-concepts/connected-accounts).

custom_metadata_has.description: Custom metadata pairs by which you want to filter connected accounts. Returns connected accounts with `custom_metadata` that contains all of the provided key:value pairs.

customer_key.description: Customer key by which you want to filter connected accounts.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned connected accounts to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `connected_account_id`, `account_type`, `customer_key`, `custom_metadata`, `user_identifier.username`, `user_identifier.email` or `user_identifier.phone`.

space_id.description: ID of the space by which you want to filter connected accounts.

user_identifier_key.description: Your user ID for the user by which you want to filter connected accounts.

post.description: Simulates a connected account becoming disconnected from Seam. Only applicable for [sandbox workspaces](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces).

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account you want to simulate as disconnected.

post.description: Request a [connected account](/core-concepts/connected-accounts) sync attempt for the specified `connected_account_id`.

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account that you want to sync.

patch.description: Updates a [connected account](/core-concepts/connected-accounts).

accepted_capabilities.description: List of accepted device capabilities that restrict the types of devices that can be connected through this connected account. Valid values are `lock`, `thermostat`, `noise_sensor`, and `access_control`.

items.description:
High-level device capabilities that can be restricted in connect webviews.
  These represent the main device categories that customers can opt into.

automatically_manage_new_devices.description: Indicates whether newly-added devices should appear as [managed devices](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices).

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account that you want to update.

custom_metadata.description: Custom metadata that you want to associate with the connected account. Entirely replaces the existing custom metadata object. If a new Connect Webview contains custom metadata and is used to reconnect a connected account, the custom metadata from the Connect Webview will entirely replace the entire custom metadata object on the connected account. Supports up to 50 JSON key:value pairs. [Adding custom metadata to a connected account](/core-concepts/connected-accounts/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-connected-account) enables you to store custom information, like customer details or internal IDs from your application. Then, you can [filter connected accounts by the desired metadata](/core-concepts/connected-accounts/filtering-connected-accounts-by-custom-metadata).

customer_key.description: The customer key to associate with this connected account. If provided, the connected account and all resources under the connected account will be moved to this customer. May only be provided if the connected account is not already associated with a customer.

display_name.description: Human-readable name for the connected account, shown in the dashboard. For example, `Booking from Airbnb House 1`.

post.description: Creates a new customer portal magic link with configurable features.

_dev.description: Whether the portal is in developer mode. Only available for Seam employees.

customer_resources_filters.description: Filter configuration for resources based on their custom_metadata. Each filter specifies a field, operation, and value to match against resource custom_metadata.

field.description: The custom_metadata field name to filter on.

operation.description: The comparison operation. Currently only '=' is supported.

customization_profile_id.description: The ID of the customization profile to use for the portal.

deep_link.description: Deep link target resource for initial redirect. When set, the portal will navigate directly to the specified resource.

exclude_locale_picker.description: Whether to exclude the option to select a locale within the portal UI.

allow_access_automation_rule_customizati.description: Indicates whether the customer can customize the access automation rules for their properties.

allow_climate_automation_rule_customizat.description: Indicates whether the customer can customize the climate automation rules for their properties.

allow_instant_key_customization.description: Indicates whether the customer can customize the Instant Key profile for their properties.

exclude.description: Whether to exclude this feature from the portal.

connect.description: Configuration for the connect accounts feature.

accepted_providers.description: List of provider keys to allow for the connect feature. These providers will be shown when the customer tries to connect an account.

exclude.description: Whether to exclude this feature from the portal.

excluded_providers.description: List of provider keys to exclude from the connect feature. These providers will not be shown when the customer tries to connect an account.

events.description: Configuration for event type filtering in the manage feature.

allowed_events.description: List of event types to show in the events filter. When set, only these event types will be available. Leave empty to show all events.

default_events.description: List of event types that are pre-selected in the events filter when the user first loads the events tab.

exclude.description: Whether to exclude this feature from the portal.

exclude_reservation_management.description: Indicates whether the customer can manage reservations for their properties.

get.description:
Returns a specified [device](/core-concepts/devices).

You must specify either `device_id` or `name`.

schema.description: ID of the device that you want to get.
Name of the device that you want to get.

post.description:
Returns a specified [device](/core-concepts/devices).

You must specify either `device_id` or `name`.

device_id.description: ID of the device that you want to get.

name.description: Name of the device that you want to get.

  "device_id": "a75bff05-29a3-4215-a09f-2156c52a4ac7"
}
EOF

get.description: Returns a list of all [devices](/core-concepts/devices).

schema.description: Customer key for which you want to list devices.
ID of the connected account for which you want to list devices.
Array of IDs of the connected accounts for which you want to list devices.
ID of the Connect Webview for which you want to list devices.
Device type for which you want to list devices.

oneOf.description: Device type for smartlocks.
Device type for keys.
Device type for noise sensors.
Device type for thermostats.
Device type for phones.
Device type for cameras.

schema.description: Array of device types for which you want to list devices.

oneOf.description: Device type for smartlocks.
Device type for keys.
Device type for noise sensors.
Device type for thermostats.
Device type for phones.
Device type for cameras.

schema.description: Manufacturer for which you want to list devices.
Array of device IDs for which you want to list devices.
Numerical limit on the number of devices to return.
Timestamp by which to limit returned devices. Returns devices created before this timestamp.
Your own internal user ID for the user for which you want to list devices.
Set of key:value [custom metadata](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) pairs for which you want to list devices.
Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.
ID of the space for which you want to list devices.

String for which to search. Filters returned devices to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `device_id` (full or partial UUID prefix, minimum 4 characters), `connected_account_id`, `display_name`, `custom_metadata` or `location.location_name`.

post.description: Returns a list of all [devices](/core-concepts/devices).

connect_webview_id.description: ID of the Connect Webview for which you want to list devices.

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account for which you want to list devices.

connected_account_ids.description: Array of IDs of the connected accounts for which you want to list devices.

created_before.description: Timestamp by which to limit returned devices. Returns devices created before this timestamp.

custom_metadata_has.description: Set of key:value [custom metadata](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) pairs for which you want to list devices.

customer_key.description: Customer key for which you want to list devices.

device_ids.description: Array of device IDs for which you want to list devices.

device_type.description: Device type for which you want to list devices.

oneOf.description: Device type for smartlocks.
Device type for keys.
Device type for noise sensors.
Device type for thermostats.
Device type for phones.
Device type for cameras.

device_types.description: Array of device types for which you want to list devices.

oneOf.description: Device type for smartlocks.
Device type for keys.
Device type for noise sensors.
Device type for thermostats.
Device type for phones.
Device type for cameras.

limit.description: Numerical limit on the number of devices to return.

manufacturer.description: Manufacturer for which you want to list devices.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned devices to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `device_id` (full or partial UUID prefix, minimum 4 characters), `connected_account_id`, `display_name`, `custom_metadata` or `location.location_name`.

space_id.description: ID of the space for which you want to list devices.

user_identifier_key.description: Your own internal user ID for the user for which you want to list devices.

  connected_account_id: "a0b1c2d3-e4f5-6a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d",

});

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --include --request POST "https://connect.getseam.com/devices/list" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $SEAM_API_KEY" \
  --json @- <<EOF
{
  "connected_account_id": "a0b1c2d3-e4f5-6a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d",
  "limit": 50
}
EOF
