get.description:
Returns a list of all device providers.

The information that this endpoint returns for each provider includes a set of [capability flags](/capability-guides/device-and-system-capabilities#capability-flags), such as `device_provider.can_remotely_unlock`. If at least one supported device from a provider has a specific capability, the corresponding capability flag is `true`.

When you create a [Connect Webview](/core-concepts/connect-webviews), you can customize the providers—that is, the brands—that it displays. In the `/connect_webviews/create` request, include the desired set of device provider keys in the `accepted_providers` parameter. See also [Customize the Brands to Display in Your Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/customizing-connect-webviews#customize-the-brands-to-display-in-your-connect-webviews).

schema.description: Category for which you want to list providers.

post.description:
Returns a list of all device providers.

The information that this endpoint returns for each provider includes a set of [capability flags](/capability-guides/device-and-system-capabilities#capability-flags), such as `device_provider.can_remotely_unlock`. If at least one supported device from a provider has a specific capability, the corresponding capability flag is `true`.

When you create a [Connect Webview](/core-concepts/connect-webviews), you can customize the providers—that is, the brands—that it displays. In the `/connect_webviews/create` request, include the desired set of device provider keys in the `accepted_providers` parameter. See also [Customize the Brands to Display in Your Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews/customizing-connect-webviews#customize-the-brands-to-display-in-your-connect-webviews).

provider_category.description: Category for which you want to list providers.

x-codeSamples.source:
seam.devices.list_device_providers()

# [
    DeviceProvider(
        can_program_online_access_codes=true,
        can_remotely_unlock=true,
        device_provider_name="akiles",
        display_name="Akiles",
        image_url="https://connect.getseam.com/_next/image?url=https://connect.getseam.com/assets/images/logos/akiles.png&q=75&w=128",
        provider_categories=["stable", "consumer_smartlocks"],
    )
]

post.description: Updates provider-specific metadata for devices.

devices.description: Array of devices with provider metadata to update

device_id.description: ID of the device to update

time_zone.description: IANA timezone for the Omnitec device
IANA timezone for the Schlage device
IANA timezone for the Ultraloq device

post.description: Simulates connecting a device to Seam. Only applicable for [sandbox devices](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces). See also [Testing Your App Against Device Disconnection and Removal](/core-concepts/devices/testing-your-app-against-device-disconnection-and-removal).

device_id.description: ID of the device that you want to simulate connecting to Seam.

post.description:
Simulates bringing the Wi‑Fi hub (bridge) back online for a device.
Only applicable for sandbox workspaces and currently
implemented for August and TTLock locks.
This will clear the `hub_disconnected` error on the device.

device_id.description: ID of the device whose hub you want to reconnect.

post.description: Simulates disconnecting a device from Seam. Only applicable for [sandbox devices](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces). See also [Testing Your App Against Device Disconnection and Removal](/core-concepts/devices/testing-your-app-against-device-disconnection-and-removal).

device_id.description: ID of the device that you want to simulate disconnecting from Seam.

post.description:
Simulates taking the Wi‑Fi hub (bridge) offline for a device.
Only applicable for sandbox workspaces and currently
implemented for August, TTLock, and IglooHome devices.
This will set the `hub_disconnected` error on the device, or mark the
IglooHome bridge offline in sandbox.

device_id.description: ID of the device whose hub you want to disconnect.

post.description:
Toggle the simulated Nuki Smart Hosting subscription for a device (sandbox only).
Send `is_expired: true` to simulate an expired subscription, or `false` to simulate an active subscription.
The actual device error is created/cleared by the poller after this state change.

Simulates removing a device from Seam. Only applicable for [sandbox devices](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces). See also [Testing Your App Against Device Disconnection and Removal](/core-concepts/devices/testing-your-app-against-device-disconnection-and-removal).

device_id.description: ID of the device that you want to simulate removing from Seam.

get.description:
Returns a specified [unmanaged device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices).

An unmanaged device has a limited set of visible properties and a subset of supported events. You cannot control an unmanaged device. Any [access codes](/low-level-apis/smart-locks/access-codes/migrating-existing-access-codes) on an unmanaged device are unmanaged. To control an unmanaged device with Seam, [convert it to a managed device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices#convert-an-unmanaged-device-to-managed).

You must specify either `device_id` or `name`.

schema.description: ID of the unmanaged device that you want to get.
Name of the unmanaged device that you want to get.

post.description:
Returns a specified [unmanaged device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices).

An unmanaged device has a limited set of visible properties and a subset of supported events. You cannot control an unmanaged device. Any [access codes](/low-level-apis/smart-locks/access-codes/migrating-existing-access-codes) on an unmanaged device are unmanaged. To control an unmanaged device with Seam, [convert it to a managed device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices#convert-an-unmanaged-device-to-managed).

You must specify either `device_id` or `name`.

device_id.description: ID of the unmanaged device that you want to get.

name.description: Name of the unmanaged device that you want to get.

get.description:
Returns a list of all [unmanaged devices](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices).

An unmanaged device has a limited set of visible properties and a subset of supported events. You cannot control an unmanaged device. Any [access codes](/low-level-apis/smart-locks/access-codes/migrating-existing-access-codes) on an unmanaged device are unmanaged. To control an unmanaged device with Seam, [convert it to a managed device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices#convert-an-unmanaged-device-to-managed).

schema.description: Customer key for which you want to list devices.
ID of the connected account for which you want to list devices.
Array of IDs of the connected accounts for which you want to list devices.
ID of the Connect Webview for which you want to list devices.
Device type for which you want to list devices.

oneOf.description: Device type for smartlocks.
Device type for keys.
Device type for noise sensors.
Device type for thermostats.
Device type for phones.
Device type for cameras.

schema.description: Array of device types for which you want to list devices.

oneOf.description: Device type for smartlocks.
Device type for keys.
Device type for noise sensors.
Device type for thermostats.
Device type for phones.
Device type for cameras.

schema.description: Manufacturer for which you want to list devices.
Array of device IDs for which you want to list devices.
Timestamp by which to limit returned devices. Returns devices created before this timestamp.
Your own internal user ID for the user for which you want to list devices.
Set of key:value [custom metadata](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) pairs for which you want to list devices.
Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.
ID of the space for which you want to list devices.

String for which to search. Filters returned devices to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `device_id` (full or partial UUID prefix, minimum 4 characters), `connected_account_id`, `display_name`, `custom_metadata` or `location.location_name`.
Numerical limit on the number of devices to return.

post.description:
Returns a list of all [unmanaged devices](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices).

An unmanaged device has a limited set of visible properties and a subset of supported events. You cannot control an unmanaged device. Any [access codes](/low-level-apis/smart-locks/access-codes/migrating-existing-access-codes) on an unmanaged device are unmanaged. To control an unmanaged device with Seam, [convert it to a managed device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices#convert-an-unmanaged-device-to-managed).

connect_webview_id.description: ID of the Connect Webview for which you want to list devices.

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account for which you want to list devices.

connected_account_ids.description: Array of IDs of the connected accounts for which you want to list devices.

created_before.description: Timestamp by which to limit returned devices. Returns devices created before this timestamp.

custom_metadata_has.description: Set of key:value [custom metadata](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) pairs for which you want to list devices.

customer_key.description: Customer key for which you want to list devices.

device_ids.description: Array of device IDs for which you want to list devices.

device_type.description: Device type for which you want to list devices.

oneOf.description: Device type for smartlocks.
Device type for keys.
Device type for noise sensors.
Device type for thermostats.
Device type for phones.
Device type for cameras.

device_types.description: Array of device types for which you want to list devices.

oneOf.description: Device type for smartlocks.
Device type for keys.
Device type for noise sensors.
Device type for thermostats.
Device type for phones.
Device type for cameras.

limit.description: Numerical limit on the number of devices to return.

manufacturer.description: Manufacturer for which you want to list devices.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned devices to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `device_id` (full or partial UUID prefix, minimum 4 characters), `connected_account_id`, `display_name`, `custom_metadata` or `location.location_name`.

space_id.description: ID of the space for which you want to list devices.

user_identifier_key.description: Your own internal user ID for the user for which you want to list devices.

patch.description:
Updates a specified [unmanaged device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices). To convert an unmanaged device to managed, set `is_managed` to `true`.

An unmanaged device has a limited set of visible properties and a subset of supported events. You cannot control an unmanaged device. Any [access codes](/low-level-apis/smart-locks/access-codes/migrating-existing-access-codes) on an unmanaged device are unmanaged. To control an unmanaged device with Seam, [convert it to a managed device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices#convert-an-unmanaged-device-to-managed).

custom_metadata.description: Custom metadata that you want to associate with the device. Supports up to 50 JSON key:value pairs.

device_id.description: ID of the unmanaged device that you want to update.

is_managed.description: Indicates whether the device is managed. Set this parameter to `true` to convert an unmanaged device to managed.

post.description:
Updates a specified [unmanaged device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices). To convert an unmanaged device to managed, set `is_managed` to `true`.

An unmanaged device has a limited set of visible properties and a subset of supported events. You cannot control an unmanaged device. Any [access codes](/low-level-apis/smart-locks/access-codes/migrating-existing-access-codes) on an unmanaged device are unmanaged. To control an unmanaged device with Seam, [convert it to a managed device](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices#convert-an-unmanaged-device-to-managed).

custom_metadata.description: Custom metadata that you want to associate with the device. Supports up to 50 JSON key:value pairs.

device_id.description: ID of the unmanaged device that you want to update.

is_managed.description: Indicates whether the device is managed. Set this parameter to `true` to convert an unmanaged device to managed.

patch.description:
Updates a specified [device](/core-concepts/devices).

You can add or change [custom metadata](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) for a device, change the device's name, or [convert a managed device to unmanaged](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices).

custom_metadata.description: Custom metadata that you want to associate with the device. Supports up to 50 JSON key:value pairs. [Adding custom metadata to a device](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) enables you to store custom information, like customer details or internal IDs from your application. Then, you can [filter devices by the desired metadata](/core-concepts/devices/filtering-devices-by-custom-metadata).

device_id.description: ID of the device that you want to update.

is_managed.description: Indicates whether the device is managed. To unmanage a device, set `is_managed` to `false`.

name.description: Name for the device.
Name for the device.

post.description:
Updates a specified [device](/core-concepts/devices).

You can add or change [custom metadata](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) for a device, change the device's name, or [convert a managed device to unmanaged](/core-concepts/devices/managed-and-unmanaged-devices).

custom_metadata.description: Custom metadata that you want to associate with the device. Supports up to 50 JSON key:value pairs. [Adding custom metadata to a device](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) enables you to store custom information, like customer details or internal IDs from your application. Then, you can [filter devices by the desired metadata](/core-concepts/devices/filtering-devices-by-custom-metadata).

device_id.description: ID of the device that you want to update.

is_managed.description: Indicates whether the device is managed. To unmanage a device, set `is_managed` to `false`.

name.description: Name for the device.

post.description: Locks a [lock](/low-level-apis/smart-locks). See also [Locking and Unlocking Smart Locks](/low-level-apis/smart-locks/lock-and-unlock).

device_id.description: ID of the lock that you want to lock.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL

post.description: Simulates the entry of a code on a keypad. You can only perform this action for [August](/device-and-system-integration-guides/august-locks) devices within [sandbox workspaces](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces).

code.description: Code that you want to simulate entering on a keypad.

device_id.description: ID of the device for which you want to simulate a keypad code entry.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
Seam SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
seam.locks.simulate.keypad_code_entry(
    device_id="97a7a706-05a9-405c-91e5-b03e5b9c2003", code="1234"
)

# ActionAttempt(
    action_attempt_id="9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3a2b-1c0d-9e8f7a6b5c4d",
    action_type="SIMULATE_KEYPAD_CODE_ENTRY",
    error=None,
    result={},
    status="success",
)

post.description: Simulates a manual lock action using a keypad. You can only perform this action for [August](/device-and-system-integration-guides/august-locks) devices within [sandbox workspaces](/core-concepts/workspaces#sandbox-workspaces).

device_id.description: ID of the device for which you want to simulate a manual lock action using a keypad.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

post.summary: Simulate a Manual Lock Action Using a Keypad
post.x-title: Simulate a Manual Lock Action Using a Keypad

x-codeSamples.label: cURL

post.description: Unlocks a [lock](/low-level-apis/smart-locks). See also [Locking and Unlocking Smart Locks](/low-level-apis/smart-locks/lock-and-unlock).

device_id.description: ID of the lock that you want to unlock.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
Seam SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
seam.locks.unlock_door(device_id="be047431-bf00-4da6-9fc7-0a7796a9b57f")

# ActionAttempt(
    action_attempt_id="5f4e3d2c-1b0a-9f8e-7d6c-5b4a3c2d1e0f",
    action_type="UNLOCK_DOOR",
    error=None,
    result={"was_confirmed_by_device": false},
    status="success",
)

get.description: Returns a list of all [noise sensors](/capability-guides/noise-sensors).

schema.description: Customer key for which you want to list devices.
ID of the connected account for which you want to list devices.
Array of IDs of the connected accounts for which you want to list devices.
ID of the Connect Webview for which you want to list devices.
Device type of the noise sensors that you want to list.
Device types of the noise sensors that you want to list.

schema.description: Manufacturers of the noise sensors that you want to list.

created_before.description: Timestamp by which to limit returned devices. Returns devices created before this timestamp.

post.description: Creates a new [noise threshold](/capability-guides/noise-sensors/configure-noise-threshold-settings) for a [noise sensor](/capability-guides/noise-sensors). Thresholds represent the limits of noise tolerated at a property, which can be customized for each hour of the day. Each device has its own default thresholds, but you can use the Seam API to modify them.

device_id.description: ID of the device for which you want to create a noise threshold.

ends_daily_at.description: Time at which the new noise threshold should become inactive daily.

name.description: Name of the new noise threshold.

noise_threshold_decibels.description: Noise level in decibels for the new noise threshold.

noise_threshold_nrs.description: Noise level in Noiseaware Noise Risk Score (NRS) for the new noise threshold. This parameter is only relevant for [Noiseaware sensors](/device-and-system-integration-guides/noiseaware-sensors).

starts_daily_at.description: Time at which the new noise threshold should become active daily.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

x-codeSamples.label: Seam SDK
cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --include --request POST "https://connect.getseam.com/noise_sensors/noise_thresholds/create" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $SEAM_API_KEY" \
  --json @- <<EOF
{
  "device_id": "8282891b-c4da-4239-8f01-56089d44b80d",
  "name": "My Noise Sensor",
  "starts_daily_at": "2025-06-20T18:29:57.000Z",
  "ends_daily_at": "2025-06-19T12:38:44.000Z",
  "noise_threshold_decibels": 50,
  "noise_threshold_nrs": 40
}
EOF

noise_threshold_id.description: ID of the noise threshold that you want to delete.

post.description: Deletes a [noise threshold](/capability-guides/noise-sensors/configure-noise-threshold-settings) from a [noise sensor](/capability-guides/noise-sensors).

device_id.description: ID of the device that contains the noise threshold that you want to delete.

noise_threshold_id.description: ID of the noise threshold that you want to delete.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

get.description: Returns a specified [noise threshold](/capability-guides/noise-sensors/configure-noise-threshold-settings) for a [noise sensor](/capability-guides/noise-sensors).

schema.description: ID of the noise threshold that you want to get.

post.description: Returns a specified [noise threshold](/capability-guides/noise-sensors/configure-noise-threshold-settings) for a [noise sensor](/capability-guides/noise-sensors).

noise_threshold_id.description: ID of the noise threshold that you want to get.

get.description: Returns a list of all [noise thresholds](/capability-guides/noise-sensors/configure-noise-threshold-settings) for a [noise sensor](/capability-guides/noise-sensors).

schema.description: ID of the device for which you want to list noise thresholds.

Enables you to limit the returned noise thresholds by whether they are programmed on the noise sensor. To list only noise thresholds that are programmed on the noise sensor, set this parameter to `true`. To list only noise thresholds that are not programmed on the noise sensor, se this parameter to `false`.
schema.x-undocumented: Not sure if this parameter is supported or what it does.

post.description: Returns a list of all [noise thresholds](/capability-guides/noise-sensors/configure-noise-threshold-settings) for a [noise sensor](/capability-guides/noise-sensors).

device_id.description: ID of the device for which you want to list noise thresholds.

is_programmed.description: Enables you to limit the returned noise thresholds by whether they are programmed on the noise sensor. To list only noise thresholds that are programmed on the noise sensor, set this parameter to `true`. To list only noise thresholds that are not programmed on the noise sensor, se this parameter to `false`.
is_programmed.x-undocumented: Not sure if this parameter is supported or what it does.

post.description: Creates a [climate preset](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-climate-presets) for a specified [thermostat](/capability-guides/thermostats).

climate_preset_key.description: Unique key to identify the [climate preset](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-climate-presets).

climate_preset_mode.description: The climate preset mode for the thermostat, based on the available climate preset modes reported by the device.

cooling_set_point_celsius.description: Temperature to which the thermostat should cool (in °C). See also [Set Points](/capability-guides/thermostats/understanding-thermostat-concepts/set-points).

cooling_set_point_fahrenheit.description: Temperature to which the thermostat should cool (in °F). See also [Set Points](/capability-guides/thermostats/understanding-thermostat-concepts/set-points).

device_id.description: ID of the thermostat device for which you want create a climate preset.

ecobee_metadata.description: Metadata specific to the Ecobee climate, if applicable.

climate_ref.description: Reference to the Ecobee climate, if applicable.

is_optimized.description: Indicates if the climate preset is optimized by Ecobee.

owner.description: Indicates whether the climate preset is owned by the user or the system.

heating_set_point_celsius.description: Temperature to which the thermostat should heat (in °C). See also [Set Points](/capability-guides/thermostats/understanding-thermostat-concepts/set-points).

heating_set_point_fahrenheit.description: Temperature to which the thermostat should heat (in °F). See also [Set Points](/capability-guides/thermostats/understanding-thermostat-concepts/set-points).

manual_override_allowed.description: Indicates whether a person at the thermostat or using the API can change the thermostat's settings.

name.description: User-friendly name to identify the [climate preset](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-climate-presets).

x-codeSamples.label: Seam SDK
cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --include --request POST "https://connect.getseam.com/thermostats/create_climate_preset" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $SEAM_API_KEY" \
  --json @- <<EOF
{
  "device_id": "ba9b816d-c255-46b9-a16d-971e6f535dd3",
  "manual_override_allowed": true,
  "climate_preset_key": "Occupied",
  "name": "Occupied",
  "fan_mode_setting": "auto",
  "hvac_mode_setting": "heat_cool",
  "cooling_set_point_celsius": 25,
  "heating_set_point_celsius": 20
}
EOF

# Response:
# {}

post.description: Creates a new thermostat daily program. A daily program consists of a set of periods, where each period includes a start time and the key of a configured climate preset. Once you have defined a daily program, you can assign it to one or more days within a weekly program.

device_id.description: ID of the thermostat device for which you want to create a daily program.

name.description: Name of the thermostat daily program.

periods.description: Array of thermostat daily program periods.

items.description: Period for a thermostat daily program. Consists of a starts at time and the key that identifies the configured climate preset to apply at the starting time.

climate_preset_key.description: Key of the [climate preset](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-climate-presets) to activate at the `starts_at_time`.

starts_at_time.description: Time at which the thermostat daily program period starts, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

x-codeSamples.label: Seam SDK
cURL
Seam SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
seam.thermostats.daily_programs.create(
    device_id="cc2d0fb9-1f5f-410f-80f1-a64b699de82a",
    name="Weekday Program",
    periods=[
        {"starts_at_time": "07:00:00", "climate_preset_key": "Home"},
        {"starts_at_time": "09:00:00", "climate_preset_key": "Away"},
        {"starts_at_time": "16:00:00", "climate_preset_key": "Home"},
        {"starts_at_time": "22:30:00", "climate_preset_key": "Sleep"},
    ],
)

get.description: Returns a list of all [thermostats](/capability-guides/thermostats).

schema.description: Customer key for which you want to list devices.
ID of the connected account for which you want to list devices.
Array of IDs of the connected accounts for which you want to list devices.
ID of the Connect Webview for which you want to list devices.
Device type by which you want to filter thermostat devices.
Array of device types by which you want to filter thermostat devices.

items.description: Device type for thermostats.

schema.description: Manufacturer by which you want to filter thermostat devices.
Array of device IDs for which you want to list devices.
Numerical limit on the number of devices to return.
Timestamp by which to limit returned devices. Returns devices created before this timestamp.
Your own internal user ID for the user for which you want to list devices.
Set of key:value [custom metadata](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) pairs for which you want to list devices.
Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.
ID of the space for which you want to list devices.

String for which to search. Filters returned devices to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `device_id` (full or partial UUID prefix, minimum 4 characters), `connected_account_id`, `display_name`, `custom_metadata` or `location.location_name`.

post.description: Returns a list of all [thermostats](/capability-guides/thermostats).

connect_webview_id.description: ID of the Connect Webview for which you want to list devices.

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account for which you want to list devices.

connected_account_ids.description: Array of IDs of the connected accounts for which you want to list devices.

created_before.description: Timestamp by which to limit returned devices. Returns devices created before this timestamp.

custom_metadata_has.description: Set of key:value [custom metadata](/core-concepts/devices/adding-custom-metadata-to-a-device) pairs for which you want to list devices.

customer_key.description: Customer key for which you want to list devices.

device_ids.description: Array of device IDs for which you want to list devices.

device_type.description: Device type by which you want to filter thermostat devices.

device_types.description: Array of device types by which you want to filter thermostat devices.

items.description: Device type for thermostats.

limit.description: Numerical limit on the number of devices to return.

manufacturer.description: Manufacturer by which you want to filter thermostat devices.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned devices to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `device_id` (full or partial UUID prefix, minimum 4 characters), `connected_account_id`, `display_name`, `custom_metadata` or `location.location_name`.

space_id.description: ID of the space for which you want to list devices.

user_identifier_key.description: Your own internal user ID for the user for which you want to list devices.

post.description: Sets a specified [thermostat](/capability-guides/thermostats) to ["off" mode](/capability-guides/thermostats/configure-current-climate-settings).

device_id.description: ID of the thermostat device that you want to set to off mode.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL

post.description: Creates a new [thermostat schedule](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-thermostat-schedules) for a specified [thermostat](/capability-guides/thermostats).

climate_preset_key.description: Key of the [climate preset](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-climate-presets) to use for the new thermostat schedule.

device_id.description: ID of the thermostat device for which you want to create a schedule.

ends_at.description: Date and time at which the new thermostat schedule ends, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

is_override_allowed.description: Indicates whether a person at the thermostat or using the API can change the thermostat's settings while the new schedule is active. See also [Specifying Manual Override Permissions](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-thermostat-schedules#specifying-manual-override-permissions).

max_override_period_minutes.description: Number of minutes for which a person at the thermostat or using the API can change the thermostat's settings after the activation of the scheduled climate preset. See also [Specifying Manual Override Permissions](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-thermostat-schedules#specifying-manual-override-permissions).

name.description: Name of the thermostat schedule.

starts_at.description: Date and time at which the new thermostat schedule starts, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

x-codeSamples.label: Seam SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
await seam.thermostats.schedules.create({
  device_id: "d710aa35-232d-442b-a817-c28045de1c74",
  name: "Jane's Stay",
  climate_preset_key: "Occupied",
  max_override_period_minutes: 90,
  starts_at: "2025-06-19T15:00:00.000Z",

patch.description: Updates a specified [thermostat schedule](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-thermostat-schedules).

climate_preset_key.description: Key of the [climate preset](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-climate-presets) to use for the thermostat schedule.

ends_at.description: Date and time at which the thermostat schedule ends, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

is_override_allowed.description: Indicates whether a person at the thermostat or using the API can change the thermostat's settings while the schedule is active. See also [Specifying Manual Override Permissions](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-thermostat-schedules#specifying-manual-override-permissions).

max_override_period_minutes.description: Number of minutes for which a person at the thermostat or using the API can change the thermostat's settings after the activation of the scheduled climate preset. See also [Specifying Manual Override Permissions](/capability-guides/thermostats/creating-and-managing-thermostat-schedules#specifying-manual-override-permissions).

name.description: Name of the thermostat schedule.

starts_at.description: Date and time at which the thermostat schedule starts, in [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format.

thermostat_schedule_id.description: ID of the thermostat schedule that you want to update.

patch.description: Sets a [temperature threshold](/capability-guides/thermostats/setting-and-monitoring-temperature-thresholds) for a specified thermostat. Seam emits a `thermostat.temperature_threshold_exceeded` event and adds a warning on a thermostat if it reports a temperature outside the threshold range.

device_id.description: ID of the thermostat device for which you want to set a temperature threshold.

lower_limit_celsius.description: Lower temperature limit in in °C. Seam alerts you if the reported temperature is lower than this value. You can specify either `lower_limit` but not both.

lower_limit_fahrenheit.description: Lower temperature limit in in °F. Seam alerts you if the reported temperature is lower than this value. You can specify either `lower_limit` but not both.

upper_limit_celsius.description: Upper temperature limit in in °C. Seam alerts you if the reported temperature is higher than this value. You can specify either `upper_limit` but not both.

upper_limit_fahrenheit.description: Upper temperature limit in in °C. Seam alerts you if the reported temperature is higher than this value. You can specify either `upper_limit` but not both.

post.description: Sets a [temperature threshold](/capability-guides/thermostats/setting-and-monitoring-temperature-thresholds) for a specified thermostat. Seam emits a `thermostat.temperature_threshold_exceeded` event and adds a warning on a thermostat if it reports a temperature outside the threshold range.

device_id.description: ID of the thermostat device for which you want to set a temperature threshold.

lower_limit_celsius.description: Lower temperature limit in in °C. Seam alerts you if the reported temperature is lower than this value. You can specify either `lower_limit` but not both.

lower_limit_fahrenheit.description: Lower temperature limit in in °F. Seam alerts you if the reported temperature is lower than this value. You can specify either `lower_limit` but not both.

upper_limit_celsius.description: Upper temperature limit in in °C. Seam alerts you if the reported temperature is higher than this value. You can specify either `upper_limit` but not both.

upper_limit_fahrenheit.description: Upper temperature limit in in °C. Seam alerts you if the reported temperature is higher than this value. You can specify either `upper_limit` but not both.

post.description: Simulates having adjusted the [HVAC mode](/capability-guides/thermostats/understanding-thermostat-concepts/hvac-mode) for a [thermostat](/capability-guides/thermostats). See also [Testing Your Thermostat App with Simulate Endpoints](/capability-guides/thermostats/testing-your-thermostat-app-with-simulate-endpoints).

hvac_mode.description: HVAC mode that you want to simulate.

cooling_set_point_celsius.description: Cooling [set point](/capability-guides/thermostats/understanding-thermostat-concepts/set-points) in °C that you want to simulate. You must set `cooling_set_point_celsius` or `cooling_set_point_fahrenheit`.

cooling_set_point_fahrenheit.description: Cooling [set point](/capability-guides/thermostats/understanding-thermostat-concepts/set-points) in °F that you want to simulate. You must set `cooling_set_point_fahrenheit` or `cooling_set_point_celsius`.

owner.description: Indicates whether the climate preset is owned by the user or the system.

post.description: Updates the thermostat weekly program for a thermostat device. To configure a weekly program, specify the ID of the daily program that you want to use for each day of the week. When you update a weekly program, the set of programs that you specify overwrites any previous weekly program for the thermostat.

device_id.description: ID of the thermostat device for which you want to update the weekly program.

friday_program_id.description: ID of the thermostat daily program to run on Fridays.

monday_program_id.description: ID of the thermostat daily program to run on Mondays.

saturday_program_id.description: ID of the thermostat daily program to run on Saturdays.

sunday_program_id.description: ID of the thermostat daily program to run on Sundays.

thursday_program_id.description: ID of the thermostat daily program to run on Thursdays.

tuesday_program_id.description: ID of the thermostat daily program to run on Tuesdays.

wednesday_program_id.description: ID of the thermostat daily program to run on Wednesdays.

action_attempt.description: Tracks the progress of this operation. Poll using the action_attempt_id.

post.description:
Adds a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) to a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

You must specify either `user_identity_id` or `user_identity_key` to identify the user identity.

If `user_identity_key` is provided, but the user identity doesn't exist, a new user identity will be created automatically using information from the ACS user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to add to the user identity.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity to which you want to add an access system user.

user_identity_key.description: Key of the user identity to which you want to add an access system user.

post.summary: Add an ACS User to a User Identity
post.x-title: Add an ACS User to a User Identity

put.description:
Adds a specified [access system user](/low-level-apis/access-systems/user-management) to a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

You must specify either `user_identity_id` or `user_identity_key` to identify the user identity.

If `user_identity_key` is provided, but the user identity doesn't exist, a new user identity will be created automatically using information from the ACS user.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that you want to add to the user identity.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity to which you want to add an access system user.

user_identity_key.description: Key of the user identity to which you want to add an access system user.

put.x-title: Add an ACS User to a User Identity

post.description: Creates a new [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

acs_system_ids.description: List of access system IDs to associate with the new user identity through access system users. If there's no user with the same email address or phone number in the specified access systems, a new access system user is created. If there is an existing user with the same email or phone number in the specified access systems, the user is linked to the user identity.

email_address.description: Unique email address for the new user identity.

full_name.description: Full name of the user associated with the new user identity.

phone_number.description: Unique phone number for the new user identity in E.164 format (for example, +15555550100).

user_identity_key.description: Unique key for the new user identity.

x-codeSamples.label: Seam SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
await seam.userIdentities.create({
  user_identity_key: "61c6c8ec-21ac-4d1d-be02-688889c66d8c",
  email_address: "jane@example.com",
  phone_number: "+15551234567",
  full_name: "Jane Doe",
  acs_system_ids: ["c359cba2-8ef2-47fc-bee0-1c7c2a886339"],
});

/*
{
  "created_at": "2025-06-16T16:54:17.946546Z",
  "display_name": "Jane Doe",
  "email_address": "jane@example.com",
  "errors": [],
  "full_name": "Jane Doe",
  "phone_number": "+15551234567",
  "user_identity_id": "43947360-cdc8-4db6-8b22-e079416d1d8b",
  "user_identity_key": "jane_doe",
  "warnings": [],
  "workspace_id": "b7e0a4e0-1044-4319-9a0b-42b642b68c7f"
}
*/

delete.description: Deletes a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity). This deletes the user identity and all associated resources, including any [credentials](/api/acs/credentials), [acs users](/api/acs/users) and [client sessions](/api/client_sessions).

schema.description: ID of the user identity that you want to delete.

post.description: Deletes a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity). This deletes the user identity and all associated resources, including any [credentials](/api/acs/credentials), [acs users](/api/acs/users) and [client sessions](/api/client_sessions).

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to delete.

post.description: Generates a new [instant key](/capability-guides/instant-keys) for a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

max_use_count.description: Maximum number of times the instant key can be used. Default: 1.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to generate an instant key.

get.description: Returns a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

post.description: Returns a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to get.

post.description: Grants a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) access to a specified [device](/core-concepts/devices/).

device_id.description: ID of the managed device to which you want to grant access to the user identity.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to grant access to a device.

post.summary: Grant a User Identity Access to a Device
post.x-title: Grant a User Identity Access to a Device

put.description: Grants a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) access to a specified [device](/core-concepts/devices/).

device_id.description: ID of the managed device to which you want to grant access to the user identity.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity that you want to grant access to a device.

put.x-title: Grant a User Identity Access to a Device

get.description: Returns a list of all [user identities](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

schema.description: Array of user identity IDs by which to filter the list of user identities.
String for which to search. Filters returned user identities to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `full_name`, `phone_number`, `email_address` or `user_identity_id`.
`acs_system_id` of the credential manager by which you want to filter the list of user identities.
Maximum number of records to return per page.
Timestamp by which to limit returned user identities. Returns user identities created before this timestamp.
Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

post.description: Returns a list of all [user identities](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

created_before.description: Timestamp by which to limit returned user identities. Returns user identities created before this timestamp.

credential_manager_acs_system_id.description: `acs_system_id` of the credential manager by which you want to filter the list of user identities.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page.

page_cursor.description: Identifies the specific page of results to return, obtained from the previous page's `next_page_cursor`.

search.description: String for which to search. Filters returned user identities to include all records that satisfy a partial match using `full_name`, `phone_number`, `email_address` or `user_identity_id`.

user_identity_ids.description: Array of user identity IDs by which to filter the list of user identities.

get.description: Returns a list of all [devices](/core-concepts/devices) associated with a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity). This includes devices derived from the access grants assigned to the user identity and devices directly linked to the user identity.

schema.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all accessible devices.

get.x-title: List Accessible Devices for a User Identity

post.description: Returns a list of all [devices](/core-concepts/devices) associated with a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity). This includes devices derived from the access grants assigned to the user identity and devices directly linked to the user identity.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all accessible devices.

post.summary: List Accessible Devices for a User Identity
post.x-title: List Accessible Devices for a User Identity

get.description: Returns a list of all [ACS entrances](/api/acs/entrances) accessible to a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity). This includes entrances derived from the access grants assigned to the user identity and entrances accessible through ACS users linked to the user identity.

schema.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all accessible entrances.

get.x-title: List Accessible Entrances for a User Identity

post.description: Returns a list of all [ACS entrances](/api/acs/entrances) accessible to a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity). This includes entrances derived from the access grants assigned to the user identity and entrances accessible through ACS users linked to the user identity.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all accessible entrances.

post.summary: List Accessible Entrances for a User Identity
post.x-title: List Accessible Entrances for a User Identity

get.description: Returns a list of all [access systems](/low-level-apis/access-systems) associated with a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

schema.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access systems.

get.x-title: List ACS Systems Associated with a User Identity

post.description: Returns a list of all [access systems](/low-level-apis/access-systems) associated with a specified [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity).

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity for which you want to retrieve all access systems.

post.summary: List ACS Systems Associated with a User Identity
post.x-title: List ACS Systems Associated with a User Identity

get.description: Returns a specified unmanaged [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) (where is_managed = false).

schema.description: ID of the unmanaged user identity that you want to get.

user_identity.description: Represents an unmanaged user identity. Unmanaged user identities do not have keys.

acs_user_ids.description: Array of access system user IDs associated with the user identity.

created_at.description: Date and time at which the user identity was created.

email_address.description: Unique email address for the user identity.

errors.description: Array of errors associated with the user identity. Each error object within the array contains fields like "error_code" and "message." "error_code" is a string that uniquely identifies the type of error, enabling quick recognition and categorization of the issue. "message" provides a more detailed description of the error, offering insights into the issue and potentially how to rectify it.

items.description: Errors associated with the user identity.

oneOf.description: Indicates that there is an issue with an access system user associated with this user identity.

acs_system_id.description: ID of the access system that the user identity is associated with.

acs_user_id.description: ID of the access system user that has an issue.

created_at.description: Date and time at which Seam created the error.

error_code.description: Unique identifier of the type of error. Enables quick recognition and categorization of the issue.

message.description: Detailed description of the error. Provides insights into the issue and potentially how to rectify it.

phone_number.description: Unique phone number for the user identity in [E.164 format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en) (for example, +15555550100).

user_identity_id.description: ID of the user identity.

warnings.description: Array of warnings associated with the user identity. Each warning object within the array contains two fields: "warning_code" and "message." "warning_code" is a string that uniquely identifies the type of warning, enabling quick recognition and categorization of the issue. "message" provides a more detailed description of the warning, offering insights into the issue and potentially how to rectify it.

patch.description:
Updates an unmanaged [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) to make it managed.

This endpoint can only be used to convert unmanaged user identities to managed ones by setting `is_managed` to `true`. It cannot be used to convert managed user identities back to unmanaged.

is_managed.description: Must be set to true to convert the unmanaged user identity to managed.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the unmanaged user identity that you want to update.

user_identity_key.description: Unique key for the user identity. If not provided, the existing key will be preserved.

post.description:
Updates an unmanaged [user identity](/capability-guides/mobile-access/managing-mobile-app-user-accounts-with-user-identities#what-is-a-user-identity) to make it managed.

This endpoint can only be used to convert unmanaged user identities to managed ones by setting `is_managed` to `true`. It cannot be used to convert managed user identities back to unmanaged.

is_managed.description: Must be set to true to convert the unmanaged user identity to managed.

user_identity_id.description: ID of the unmanaged user identity that you want to update.

user_identity_key.description: Unique key for the user identity. If not provided, the existing key will be preserved.

curl --include --request POST "https://connect.getseam.com/user_identities/update" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $SEAM_API_KEY" \
  --json @- <<EOF
{
  "user_identity_id": "dc378ea9-358e-4999-b295-d0f3e0d5ff51",
  "user_identity_key": "jane_doe",
  "email_address": "jane@example.com",
  "phone_number": "+15551234567",
  "full_name": "Jane Doe"
}
EOF

primary_button_text_color.description: Primary button text color for [Connect Webviews](/core-concepts/connect-webviews) in the new workspace.
